alerts.message: Fixed costs €62,781/month are immovable; current month budget revenue €201,863 covers ~3.2x costs but export DSO (6-8 weeks) creates cash lag. Verify April collections on schedule.
Bemböle contract (€25K/mo) ends September 2026; plan for €25K monthly revenue loss and confirm renewal or replacement revenue by June.
Only €3,171 open AR with zero overdue invoices suggests strong collection discipline; maintain own-channel priority (3-5 day payment terms) to accelerate cash inflow.

insights.weekly_cash_risk: April has healthy margin (€201,863 revenue vs €62,781 fixed), but 6-8 week export DSO means cash from peak August revenue will arrive in September–October when Bemböle contract ends.
insights.top_action: Schedule contract renewal negotiation with Bemböle by May 31 to lock replacement revenue before Q3 cliff; simultaneously map August peak revenue collection dates to confirm September–October cash availability.
pattern_insights: Zero overdue invoices and minimal open AR (€3,171) indicates mature collection process; 62% YoY growth is not yet creating DSO or AR creep.
Export-driven model with 6-8 week DSO creates structural seasonal cash gap: peak August revenue lands as cash in October, after Q3 fixed costs and Bemböle exit occur.
insights.wc_note: Working capital is tight relative to 62% growth: large revenue swings (€282K peak August vs lower months) combined with long export DSO and fixed €62,781 monthly burn require 8-week cash buffer; Bemböle loss in September accelerates this pressure.
